title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Capesize Brazil to China
July's spikes in the number of ballasters with rates dropping throughout the month
http s://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-c3/2p6y4b/335457918?h=Nna_bjiQSRpg2HcyNikYJ3edPHd7TSuWASI7Frqv1Bk
The last days of July are still quiet, with a weaker sentiment of freight rates in all size classes, while all eyes are on the Black Sea grain market after Russia decided not to extend the agreement that allows the safe shipment of Ukrainian grain through the Black Sea ports. The third quarter of this year brings several challenges, such as the increasing number of Capesize vessels and uncertainty about the level of Chinese economic activity. State news agency Xinhua reported this week that China will step up economic policy adjustments, focusing on expanding domestic demand, boosting confidence, and avoiding risks, as the second quarter of this year brought insufficient domestic demand.

The last days of July are still quiet, with a weaker sentiment of freight rates in all size classes, while all eyes are on the Black Sea grain market after Russia decided not to extend the agreement that allows the safe shipment of Ukrainian grain through the Black Sea ports. The third quarter of this year brings several challenges, such as the increasing number of Capesize vessels and uncertainty about the level of Chinese economic activity.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes Market Rates ($/t) Weaker
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-26T115629.569.png | assets/2023-07-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-26t115629569_9ae7d6cdf210.png
A mixed week in the last week of July, where we saw signs of a relatively quiet market with a soft revival of rates in Panamax Cont- FE. It appears that momentum has remained relatively flat in the Handysize and Supramax segments.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (# vessels) Increasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-26T120143.336.png | assets/2023-07-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-26t120143336_d3b7570a9e0f.png
The last week of July held the surprising strengthening of the upward trend of the previous week, with increases across all ship size categories. Capesize SE Africa: The number of ballasters is 128, well above the annual average of 83 and up 50% from the low in week 26.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND
children.summary:
Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size TonneDays Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-26T120251.650.png | assets/2023-07-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-26t120251650_301f0c1ec979.png
In the last week of July, the downward trend continued, while the signs of recovery remain uncertain, as the rest of the summer season seems to remain quiet. Capesize: Demand growth fell to a new low in week 30, and the downward revision appears to be for early August.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports No of Vessels Increasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-26T120355.891.png | assets/2023-07-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-26t120355891_9625fc98d231.png
The fourth week of July began with an increase in congestion, but as we noted the previous week, there is a high potential for a downward correction before the close. Capesize: The current number of ship congestions dropped below 120, which is 14 lower than two weeks ago.
